# terrible_dellemc_openmanage_ome_firmware_baseline (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `baseline_description` (String) Description for the baseline being created.
- `baseline_id` (Number) ID of the existing baseline. This option is mutually exclusive with *baseline_name*.
- `baseline_name` (String) Name of the the baseline. This option is mutually exclusive with *baseline_id*.
- `catalog_name` (String) Name of the catalog to be associated with the baseline.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `device_group_names` (String) List of group names. This option is mutually exclusive with *device_ids* and *device_service_tags*.
- `device_ids` (String) List of device IDs. This option is mutually exclusive with *device_service_tags* and *device_group_names*.
- `device_service_tags` (String) List of device service tags. This option is mutually exclusive with *device_ids* and *device_group_names*.
- `downgrade_enabled` (Boolean) Indicates whether firmware downgrade is allowed for the devices in the baseline. This value will be set to `true` by default, if not provided during baseline creation.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `filter_no_reboot_required` (Boolean) Select only components with no reboot required allows to create a firmware/driver baseline that consists of only the components of the target devices that don't require a reboot of the target devices.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `is_64_bit` (Boolean) Indicates if the repository contains 64-bit DUPs. This value will be set to `true` by default, if not provided during baseline creation.
- `job_wait` (Boolean) Provides the option to wait for job completion. This option is applicable when *state* is `present`.
- `job_wait_timeout` (Number) The maximum wait time of *job_wait* in seconds. The job is tracked only for this duration. This option is applicable when *job_wait* is `true`.
- `new_baseline_name` (String) New name of the baseline.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) `present` creates or modifies a baseline. `absent` deletes an existing baseline.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `baseline_status` (String) Details of the baseline status.
- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `error_info` (String) Details of http error.
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `job_id` (Number) Job ID of the baseline task.
- `msg` (String) Overall status of the firmware baseline operation.
